articles.title: IT IS TRIVIALLY EASY TO USE REDDIT TO MANIPULATE AI SEARCH
articles.summary: AI search and deep research tools can be influenced by extremely small pieces of UGC on sites like Reddit, Wikipedia, and Quora. As little as a 13-word snippet can shift AI outputs and cause models to repeat or cite spam-like claims. These systems rely heavily on user-generated sources for retrieval, which makes them vulnerable to targeted manipulation through lightly edited or promotional posts. Well-placed comments can also distort what AI tools surface as answers.

articles.title: NEW RESEARCH SHOWS HONESTY ABOUT AI USE AT WORK IS BACKFIRING
articles.summary: AI adoption at work is near universal, with 94% of knowledge workers using it and most openly discussing it with managers and peers. New research shows disclosure carries a cost even when outputs are identical. In a controlled experiment, participants rated AI users as 10X lazier and were 24 percentage points less likely to recommend them for high-visibility work when told AI was used. Framing AI as helping the team improves ratings but still ranks below workers who do not mention AI.

articles.title: TOPICS MATTER FOR THIRD-PARTY AUTHORITY SIGNALS
articles.summary: AI pulls from a different set of trusted sources for every topic, making broad off-site authority building inefficient. Instead of spreading mentions across generic high-authority sites, brands need depth within the specific sources models already trust in their niche. AI reuses existing trust from those entities, meaning citations depend more on topical reputation and credible third-party mentions than overall backlink volume. Concentrated placements in a few top-tier, in-topic sources drive more visibility than scattered coverage.

articles.title: WE ANALYZED 137K SITES: 97% OF LLMS.TXT FILES NEVER GET READ
articles.summary: 97% of llms.txt files got zero readers in May, despite growing adoption driven by speculation about AI benefits. Of the limited traffic, 77% came from non-AI bots, with most AI access coming from coding agents like Claude Code, not search tools. Perplexity and ChatGPT together made up just 1.1% of requests, and no AI system actively looked for the file when it wasn't present.

articles.title: QUANTIFYING YOUTUBE KEYWORD OPPORTUNITIES
articles.summary: YouTube keyword research uses different metrics than traditional SEO since most views come from discovery, not search. Instead of search volume, use Median Monthly View Velocity (MMVV) to estimate demand across top videos. Competition is measured by median subscribers of ranking channels, while median video age shows how long content stays relevant. Keywords with strong view velocity, lower subscriber competition, and longer lifespan signal more potential.

articles.title: HOW G2 REVIEWS ARE BUILDING THE TRUST LAYER OF AI SEARCH IN B2B
articles.summary: ChatGPT and Perplexity are increasingly citing G2 to recommend software, even over traditional search results. G2 jumped to ~23,700 citations in Perplexity and 12,000 in ChatGPT as 71% of B2B buyers now use AI for research. Models rely on fresh reviews, ratings, and comparisons to justify answers. This makes recent, high-quality customer feedback more influential than search rankings.

articles.title: ELECTRONIC ARTS LAUNCHES EA ADVERTISING
articles.summary: EA Advertising is a new unit that lets brands appear directly inside gameplay through real-time placements like stadium signage, scoreboards, overlays, and in-game challenges. The system runs across EA's 120M+ monthly active users, with EA SPORTS FC alone generating over 1B matches per month. A new ad server enables privacy-safe targeting, alongside partnerships for live events and community activations.

articles.title: THE MODERN-DAY MICHELIN GUIDE: ANTHROPIC'S MASSIVE PROGRAMMATIC SEO OPPORTUNITY
articles.summary: This proposal argues Anthropic could replicate plays like Michelin and Guinness by using Claude to programmatically generate millions of free tools, calculators, and widgets that capture search demand. By making them embeddable, every use becomes a distribution channel, pushing users back to Claude when they want to customize. Combined with continuous iteration and user input, this “artifact gallery” could drive massive organic traffic and convert it into signups at scale.

articles.title: YOU WILL BE REPLACED BY A 19-YEAR-OLD
articles.summary: AI isn't replacing marketers, it's flattening skill gaps so experience matters less. What took a decade to learn is now cheap and accessible, making younger creators instantly competitive. Their edge isn't tools, it's instinct and audience fluency from growing up online. They take more risks and ship without overthinking. Experienced marketers get slowed down by caution. The takeaway is to unlearn that hesitation and prioritize hiring for hunger and native internet instincts over experience.

articles.title: 7 NEW-ISH BOOKS I LOVE
articles.summary: The list includes Jay Schwedelson on testing your way to standout results, Erika Heald on AI content workflows, and Austin Kleon on creative unlearning.

articles.title: FOX ACQUIRES ROKU FOR $22B TO ACCELERATE STREAMING ADVERTISING BUSINESS
articles.summary: Fox's acquisition of Roku combines content with CTV OS and first-party data from over half of US broadcast homes, signaling streaming and live sports dominance.
